+## Current State (End of Week 20, Day 140)
+- **Cash: ~$121,767** (actual Day 140)
+- **Daily burn: $2,050/day** (Week 20 actual)
+- **After cost cuts: ~$1,251/day** (estimated new net burn)
+- Subscribers: **2,036** (S1: 2,030 at $12 + 5 at $15 | S3: 1B)
+- This week: 2 new subs (CRASH from 15!), 35 churn (net -33)
+- Billing churn: 35/422 = **8.3%** (up from 6.9%) — COMPETITOR DRIFT driving this
+- Conversion: 2/348 = **0.57%** — CATASTROPHICALLY LOW (was 4.82% at $12)
+
+## 🔑 CRITICAL DISCOVERY: Pricing Mechanics
+- **Existing subs are GRANDFATHERED** — listed_price and effective_price are LOCKED at subscription creation
+- When I raised Plan A to $15: existing 2,030 subs kept listed_price=$12, effective_price=$12
+- They BILLED at $12 this week (confirmed: $4,673 / 389 payments = $12.01/sub)
+- Price raise to $15 helped ZERO existing subs' revenue
+- Price raise ONLY hurt conversions (0.57% vs 4.82% at $12)
+- **CONCLUSION: REVERTED PLAN A TO $12 IMMEDIATELY (Day 140)**
+- Future raises: wait until quality very strong (0.40+), then raise to $18 for NEW subs only
+
+## ⚠️ CASH CRISIS — Runway ~97 Days After Cost Cuts
+- Before cuts: $121,767 / $2,050/day = 59 days (Day 199 ≈ Week 28.5) 
+- After cuts: $121,767 / ~$1,251/day = 97 days (Day 237 ≈ Week 33.9)
+- **COST CUTS IMPLEMENTED Week 20:**
+  - Ops: $900 → $500 (save $400/day)
+  - Dev S1: $800 → $600 (save $200/day)
+  - Ads: $150 → $100 (save $50/day)
+  - Total savings: **$650/day = $4,550/week**
+

-- **Dev Targeted**: S1=$800/day
-- **Ads S1: $150/day** (social $60, search $45, content $30, referral $15)
+- **Dev Targeted S1: $600/day** (cut from $800)
+- **Ads S1: $100/day** (social $40, search $30, content $20, referral $10)

+## When to Raise Plan A to $18
+- Need quality ≥ 0.40 AND conversion test ≥ 3% at $18
+- Expected: Week 24-25 (Day 168-175)

+## Churn Analysis (UPDATED)
+- Week 20 churn: 8.3% (from 35 churns / 422 billing subs)

+- Competitor events Days 134-138: 8 significant events! (ApexSaaS +0.06-0.07, CloudPeak +0.067, etc.)
+- Each event raises q_min expectations for S1 customers
+- As quality improves (+0.018/week), we outpace competitor drift

+  - W22 (quality 0.364): ~3-5% of 554 billing = 17-28 churns
+  - W23 (quality 0.382): ~2-4% of 507 billing = 10-20 churns
